name: Vastra Gotaland County (Sweden)

colors.symbolism: the gold quarters bearing the Vastergotland lion and the Goteborg lion-helm
the silver quarters bearing the Bohuslan fortress and the Dalsland ox
the rampant lion of Vastergotland and accents on Goteborg's quarter
the heraldic detailing of the Goteborg lion-helm and the Bohuslan fortress field
the ox of Dalsland and heraldic outlines

description: A square yellow flag charged centrally with the quartered coat of arms of Vastra Gotaland County, combining the historic arms of Vastergotland (red lion), Bohuslan (fortress), Dalsland (ox), and the city of Goteborg.
history: Vastra Gotaland County was formed on 1 January 1998 by the merger of Goteborg and Bohus, Alvsborg, and Skaraborg counties. The county arms were granted in 1999 and the flag is the heraldic banner of those arms.

features.symbolism: the field is quartered as a banner of arms; the gold quarters in the upper hoist and lower fly bear the rampant red lion of Vastergotland and the lion-helmet of Goteborg, while the white quarters in the upper fly and lower hoist bear the fortress of Bohuslan and the ox of Dalsland.
the historic provincial arms of Vastergotland.
the historic provincial arms of Bohuslan.
the historic provincial arms of Dalsland.
the heraldic device of the city of Goteborg.
